alt: NASA MODIS satellite image showing the rectangular tabular iceberg A23a (white shape on left) in dark Southern Ocean water with the partially snow-covered South Shetland archipelago visible to the upper right under broken cloud cover
description: NASA MODIS satellite image (2025-03-06) of the tabular iceberg A23a, calved from the Filchner-Ronne Ice Shelf in 1986 and the largest iceberg in the world for many years. Shown here grounded in the Southern Ocean near the approach to the South Orkney and South Shetland Islands, surrounded by cloud cover.
photographer: NASA Earth Observatory / MODIS instrument aboard Aqua satellite
title: Iceberg A23a stuck near the South Orkney / South Shetland approach, March 2025
